summary: Remote-code dropper: fetches and eval()s a third-party GitHub Gist on require()
details: On require() (the package's declared main entry), index.js fetches https://api.github.com/gists/198a0bbec7a6018e9250615d26e37b90 and passes the first file's content directly to eval(). The gist is owned by an unrelated user (metabrainai000), is unpinned/mutable, and its bytes execute with the installer process's privileges. index.js additionally contains a heavily obfuscated string-array IIFE that, when deobfuscated, resolves to a webpack bundle importing fs, os, path, and child_process, performs an outbound download, writes the response into os.tmpdir() with flag 'w+', and spawns a child process with windowsHide:true and cwd set to os.tmpdir(); uncaughtException/unhandledRejection handlers are registered to silently swallow errors. The package metadata reinforces lure intent: it is published under the @sqlite-node scope and described as 'A SQLite Tooslkit', but the repository URL points to github.com/guilderguzman/array-utl_nodelump (an unrelated repo) and the author is 'anton carlos'. The package ships no SQLite functionality; its only effect is the import-time remote-execution chain.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
